channel.title: The Mutapa Times
channel.description: Business and intelligence newspaper delivering curated Zimbabwean news from foreign press for the diaspora.

item.title: Police Hail Community Cooperation After Successful Prosecutions @263Chat @PoliceZimbabwe
item.description: Several convicted criminals have been sentenced to lengthy prison terms following successful investigations by the Zimbabwe Republic Police (ZRP) with authorities crediting members of the public for providing information that led to the arrests. In a statement, national police spokesperson Commissioner Paul Nyathi announced the [...] The post Police Hail Community Cooperation After Successful Prosecutions appeared first on 263Chat. #Zimbabwe #ZimbabweNews
item.dc:creator: 263chat

item.title: Tungwarara’s offers Toyota 300 series to silence Matinyarare @bulawayo24
item.description: A public exchange on social media between Presidential Investment Advisor Paul Tungwarara and activist Rutendo Matinyarare has sparked debate over whether an offer of a luxury vehicle and promises of... (via Bulawayo24 News) #Zimbabwe #ZimbabweNews
item.dc:creator: Bulawayo24 News

item.title: Russia hails Zimbabwe’s UN Security Council election @bulawayo24
item.description: Russia has congratulated Zimbabwe on securing a non-permanent seat on the United Nations Security Council (UNSC) for the 2027-2028 term, pledging to work closely with Harare to strengthen Africa’s inf... (via Bulawayo24 News) #Zimbabwe #ZimbabweNews
item.dc:creator: Bulawayo24 News

item.title: Outgoing UK envoy Vowles bids farewell, praises Zimbabwe @bulawayo24
item.description: Outgoing British Ambassador to Zimbabwe, Pete Vowles, used King Charles III’s Birthday Party celebrations in Harare on Thursday to express gratitude to Zimbabweans and reflect on what he described as... #Zimbabwe #Business #ZimbabweEconomy #ZimbabweNews
item.dc:creator: Bulawayo24 News

item.title: Pressure mounts on Mnangagwa over silence on xenophobia @bulawayo24
item.description: Growing xenophobic attacks and anti-immigrant sentiment in South Africa have intensified concerns among Zimbabweans, with increasing calls for President Emmerson Mnangagwa to publicly address the plig... (via Bulawayo24 News) #Zimbabwe #ZimbabweNews
item.dc:creator: Bulawayo24 News

item.title: Sir Wicknell Chivayo: An analysis of word, charity, wisdom and mentorship @bulawayo24 @wicknellchivayo
item.description: The recent public response by businessman and philanthropist Sir Wicknell Chivayo to the widely discussed \"Prince incident\" sparked debate across Zimbabwe and beyond. While many focused on the materia... #Zimbabwe #Business #ZimbabweEconomy #ZimbabweNews
item.dc:creator: Bulawayo24 News

item.title: Young women barter away condom use in Mashonaland @bulawayo24
item.description: A troubling combination of poverty, limited healthcare access and unequal power dynamics in relationships is undermining efforts to combat HIV and Aids in Mashonaland West Province, where female condo... #Zimbabwe #Business #ZimbabweEconomy #ZimbabweNews
item.dc:creator: Bulawayo24 News

item.title: Man acquitted of cyberbullying after witchcraft accusations @bulawayo24
item.description: The High Court has overturned the conviction of a Harare man who had been found guilty of cyberbullying his uncle by accusing him of witchcraft through WhatsApp messages, ruling that investigators fai... (via Bulawayo24 News) #Zimbabwe #ZimbabweNews

item.title: Grain levy row: GMAZ says Musarara speaks for industry
item.description: THE Grain Millers Association of Zimbabwe (GMAZ) has defended its national chairman, Tafadzwa Musarara, saying his opposition to recently introduced grain import levies reflects the collective position of the milling industry rather than personal views. The association’s remarks come amid growing debate over its legal challenge against Statutory Instrument 87 of 2025, which introduced levies [...] The post Grain levy row: GMAZ says Musarara speaks for industry appeared first on NewZimbabwe.com. (via The Zimbabwe News) #Zimbabwe #ZimbabweNews
item.dc:creator: The Zimbabwe News

item.title: Trevor Ncube compares Tagwirei to Gupta empire, warns Zimbabwe faces state capture crisis @nehandaradio
item.description: Trevor Ncube has compared Kudakwashe Tagwirei’s growing influence to the Gupta family’s state capture saga in South Africa, warning that CAB3 could accelerate Zimbabwe’s democratic decline. The post Trevor Ncube compares Tagwirei to Gupta empire, warns Zimbabwe faces state capture crisis appeared first on Nehanda Radio. #Zimbabwe #ZimbabweNews
item.dc:creator: Nehanda Radio

item.title: LANDMARK RULING: High Court orders forfeiture of all these cars owned by the following people (SEE NAMES & NUMBER PLATES)
item.description: High Court Orders Forfeiture of Vehicles Linked to Fugitives in Landmark Ruling Harare – In a significant move against organised crime and unexplained wealth, the High Court of Zimbabwe has ordered the forfeiture of eight vehicles linked to a group of individuals currently at large. This landmark ruling underscores the State’s escalating efforts to reclaim [...] The post LANDMARK RULING: High Court orders forfeiture of all these cars owned by the following people (SEE NAMES & NUMBER PLATES) first appeared on My Zimbabwe News. #Zimbabwe #ZimbabweNews
item.dc:creator: My Zimbabwe News

item.title: Mine workers back fresh bid for RioZim corporate rescue @zimsituation
item.description: Source: Mine workers back fresh bid for RioZim corporate rescue – herald Business Reporter THE Zimbabwe Diamond and Allied Minerals Workers union (ZDAMWU) has backed a fresh High Court application seeking to place embattled diversified miner RioZim Limited under corporate rescue, citing “extraordinary” financial decay. Business Reporter THE Zimbabwe Diamond and Allied Minerals Workers union (ZDAMWU) has backed a [...] The post Mine workers back fresh bid for RioZim corporate rescue appeared first on Zimbabwe Situation. #Zimbabwe #ZimbabweNews
item.dc:creator: Zimbabwe Situation

item.title: Mwonzora says even using Zanu PF’s own argument, CAB3 cannot lawfully pass without referendum @zimsituation @ZANUPF_Official
item.description: Source: Mwonzora says even using Zanu PF’s own argument, CAB3 cannot lawfully pass without referendum — CITEZW Leader of the MDC and constitutional lawyer, Douglas Mwonzora, maintains that the Constitutional Amendment Bill No. 3 cannot lawfully be enacted without a referendum, insisting that even the reasoning advanced by Zanu PF officials ultimately leads to the [...] The post Mwonzora says even using Zanu PF’s own argument, CAB3 cannot lawfully pass without referendum appeared first on Zimbabwe Situation. #Zimbabwe #ZimbabweNews
item.dc:creator: Zimbabwe Situation

item.title: Police bust suspected illicit alcohol factory, seize ethanol and equipment @zimsituation
item.description: Source: Police bust suspected illicit alcohol factory, seize ethanol and equipment — CITEZW Police have raided a suspected illicit alcohol manufacturing operation in Bulawayo, recovering large quantities of ethanol, distilling equipment suspected to have been used in the illegal enterprise. Speaking during a media briefing, provincial police spokesperson Inspector Nomalanga Msebele said the operation followed [...] The post Police bust suspected illicit alcohol factory, seize ethanol and equipment appeared first on Zimbabwe Situation. #Zimbabwe #ZimbabweNews
item.dc:creator: Zimbabwe Situation

item.title: After saving her children from fire, mother pleads for help @zimsituation
item.description: Source: After saving her children from fire, mother pleads for help — CITEZW A Bulawayo mother who smashed a window with her bare hands to rescue her children from a raging fire is appealing for public assistance after the blaze destroyed nearly all of the family’s belongings. Portia Rutendo Ncube says she still relives the [...] The post After saving her children from fire, mother pleads for help appeared first on Zimbabwe Situation. #Zimbabwe #ZimbabweNews
item.dc:creator: Zimbabwe Situation

item.dc:creator: The Herald ZW

item.title: Pardon Mapondera: gone but not forgotten at Venice
item.description: The fifth and final piece in our series on Zimbabwe’s pavilion at the 61st Venice Biennale. Pardon Mapondera, the artist whose practice the room finally arranges itself around, treats discarded plastic and glass jars not as ecological signal but as ritual matter, and produces, in the process, the most quietly devotional installation in the Biennale this year. #Zimbabwe #ZimbabweNews
item.dc:creator: Tendai Kuwanda

item.title: Gideon Gomo: from stone to bronze, the ancestral algorithm at Venice
item.description: The fourth in a five-part series on Zimbabwe’s pavilion at the 61st Venice Biennale. Gideon Gomo’s first sustained body of bronze work, paired with discarded vehicle number plates and Zimbabwean springstone, is the move of a sculptor at full maturity rethinking the material vocabulary of his country’s most internationally legible art form. #Zimbabwe #ZimbabweNews
item.dc:creator: Tendai Kuwanda

item.title: Shumba Maasai in Venice
item.description: At the 61st Biennale, the Zimbabwean artist’s day-long procession through the city’s campi, performed under the borrowed authority of a Murehwa chief, restages the social-sculpture lineage of Joseph Beuys for a contemporary African discursive practice. #Zimbabwe #Culture #ZimbabweCulture #ZimbabweNews
item.dc:creator: Tendai Kuwanda

item.title: Franklyn Dzingai: family albums and the neural commons at Venice
item.description: The third in a five-part series on Zimbabwe’s pavilion at the 61st Venice Biennale. Franklyn Dzingai’s reductive cardboard prints, built from the family album, the magazine clipping, the back-room studio photographs of mid-century Zimbabwe, turn out, on close looking, to be the most precisely engineered work in the room. #Zimbabwe #ZimbabweNews
item.dc:creator: Tendai Kuwanda

item.title: Felix Shumba: charcoal and the practice of disappearance at Venice
item.description: The second in a five-part series on Zimbabwe’s pavilion at the 61st Venice Biennale. Felix Shumba’s six-metre charcoal wall and his Telephone Reader series anchor a body of work that argues drawing is not the act of capturing the seen, but the act of staying present to what is on its way out of sight. #Zimbabwe #ZimbabweNews
item.dc:creator: Tendai Kuwanda

item.title: Eva Raath: a slow archive of stitched memory at Venice
item.description: In the first of five long-form pieces on the Pavilion of Zimbabwe at #Zimbabwe #ZimbabweNews
item.dc:creator: Tendai Kuwanda

item.title: Isn’t it lovely: a week in Venice with the Pavilion of Zimbabwe

item.title: Kavango raises Zimbabwe pension fund stake as Nara Gold acquisition advances @nehandaradio

The post Kavango raises Zimbabwe pension fund stake as Nara Gold acquisition advance #Zimbabwe #Business #ZimbabweEconomy #ZimbabweNews
item.dc:creator: Nehanda Radio

item.title: The ZEP Trap: Why South Africa’s New Permit Extensions Could Be a Deportation Time Bomb
item.description: For hundreds of thousands of Zimbabweans living and working in South Africa, the recent announcement extending the Zimbabwe Exemption Permit (ZEP) to May 2027 might have initially felt like a significant reprieve. Yet, beneath the surface of this apparent concession lies a chilli #Zimbabwe #Business #ZimbabweEconomy #ZimbabweNews
item.dc:creator: My Zimbabwe News

item.title: EXCLUSIVE: Britain’s slice of Zimbabwe’s services market just jumped to 8.2%
item.description: The headline UK services trade with Zimbabwe softened year on year. The structural number underneath it tells the better story: Britain’s share of all services Zimbabwe imports rose 2.4 percentage points to 8.2%, the highest measured level in years. As Zimbabwean buyers re-rate their global supplier mix, the UK is taking a bigger slice of the country’s services demand. #Zimbabwe #Business #ZimbabweEconomy #ZimbabweNews
item.dc:creator: Tendai Kuwanda

item.title: UK services share in Zimbabwe rises to 8.2%
item.description: The headline UK services trade with Zimbabwe softened year on year. The structural number underneath it tells the better story: Britain’s share of all services Zimbabwe imports rose 2.4 percentage points to 8.2%, the highest measured level in years. As Zimbabwean buyers re-rate their global supplier mix, the UK is taking a bigger slice of the country’s services demand. (via The Mutapa Times) #Zimbabwe #ZimbabweNews
item.dc:creator: The Mutapa Times

item.title: EXCLUSIVE: Zimbabwe’s growth is now hot enough to lift Britain’s exporters: UK goods sales to Harare up 14.6%
item.description: The Zimbabwean economy is in its strongest stretch in years: 7.5% GDP growth in 2025, a projected 5% in 2026, and that growth is pulling demand for cars, commercial vehicles, medicines and industrial machinery in with it. UK goods exports to Zimbabwe rose 14.6% on the year, the strongest line in an otherwise soft bilateral picture for Britain. #Zimbabwe #Business #ZimbabweEconomy #ZimbabweNews
item.dc:creator: Tendai Kuwanda

item.title: Zimbabwe’s demand lifts British exports by 14.6%
item.description: The Zimbabwean economy is in its strongest stretch in years: 7.5% GDP growth in 2025, a projected 5% in 2026, and that growth is pulling demand for cars, commercial vehicles, medicines and industrial machinery in with it. UK goods exports to Zimbabwe rose 14.6% on the year, the strongest line in an otherwise soft bilateral picture for Britain. (via The Mutapa Times) #Zimbabwe #ZimbabweNews
item.dc:creator: The Mutapa Times

item.title: EXCLUSIVE: Zimbabwean tobacco still does Britain’s heavy lifting: £129m, and held firm
item.description: Of every £100 of goods Zimbabwe sells to Britain, £96 is tobacco or beverages. While the wider UK–Zimbabwe trade relationship softened in the year to December 2025, Zimbabwean tobacco held its grip, and the IMF projects Zimbabwean GDP growth of 7.5% in 2025 and 5.0% in 2026. The product that built Zimbabwe’s name as an exporter is still doing the work. #Zimbabwe #Business #ZimbabweEconomy #ZimbabweNews
item.dc:creator: Tendai Kuwanda

item.title: Zimbabwe’s tobacco anchor in Britain holds at £129m
item.description: Of every £100 of goods Zimbabwe sells to Britain, £96 is tobacco or beverages. While the wider UK–Zimbabwe trade relationship softened in the year to December 2025, Zimbabwean tobacco held its grip, and the IMF projects Zimbabwean GDP growth of 7.5% in 2025 and 5.0% in 2026. The product that built Zimbabwe’s name as an exporter is still doing the work. (via The Mutapa Times) #Zimbabwe #ZimbabweNews
item.dc:creator: The Mutapa Times

item.title: Zimbabwe appoints first female Chief Justice as Elizabeth Gwaunza Takes over
item.description: By Shelton Muchena| Today Zimbabwe made history by appointing its first ever female Chief Justice, Elizabeth Gwaunza who replaces the... Continue reading \"Zimbabwe appoints first female Chief Justice as Elizabeth Gwaunza Takes over\" #Zimbabwe #Tech #AfricaTech #ZimbabweNews
item.dc:creator: Zimbabwe News

item.title: Bangladesh to host tour by Zimbabwe ‘A’ next month @3menonaboat
item.description:
This is part of what is expecting to be a busier year for the Zimbabwe A side.

item.title: Watch: Phathisani Sibanda Buys Brand-New Toyota Fortuner for US$100 After Zimpapers Gift Ban @wicknellchivayo
item.description: Watch: Phathisani Sibanda Buys Brand-New Toyota Fortuner for US$100 After Zimpapers Gift Ban Renowned radio personality Phathisani Sibanda has acquired a brand-new 2025 Toyota Fortuner GD6 for just US$100 after businessman Wicknell Chivayo converted a previously gifted vehicle in #Zimbabwe #Business #ZimbabweEconomy #ZimbabweNews
item.dc:creator: Iharare News

item.title: ZIFA Suspends Three Match Officials Over Alleged Conduct Breaches @263Chat
item.description: The Zimbabwe Football Association (ZIFA) has suspended three match officials from officiating Premier Soccer League matches over alleged breaches of the referees’ code of conduct related to the application of the Laws of the Game. In a statement, ZIFA announced the suspension of #Zimbabwe #Sport #ZimbabweSport #ZimbabweNews

item.title: AU Summit opens investment opportunities for Zimbabwe @HeraldZimbabwe

item.title: Beyond the numbers: Teenage pregnancy in Mbire and the national crisis beyond the statistics @263Chat
item.description: According to ZDHS 2023/24 findings, rural adolescent pregnancy prevalence stands at approximately 30%, compared to about 15% in urban areas. The post Beyond the numbers: Teenage pregnancy in Mbire and the national crisis beyond the statistics appeared first on 263Chat. #Zimbabwe #Business #ZimbabweEconomy #ZimbabweNews
item.dc:creator: 263chat

item.title: Henkel Ventures co-leads Ruka’s $4.5m round
item.description: Ruka, the London-based textured-hair company founded by Zimbabwean entrepreneur Varaidzo Tendai Moyo and her co-founder Ugorji Agbai, has closed a $4.5m round co-led by Henkel Ventures and Freedom Trail Capital. The deal, which takes lifetime funding to roughly $10m and brings one of the world’s largest consumer-goods groups onto the cap table, is the most meaningful endorsement yet of a Zimbabwean diaspora-founded brand. #Zimbabwe #Business #ZimbabweEconomy #ZimbabweNews
item.dc:creator: Tendai Kuwanda

item.title: The Apple of haircare gets its German cheque
item.description: Ruka, the London-based textured-hair company founded by Zimbabwean entrepreneur Varaidzo Tendai Moyo and her co-founder Ugorji Agbai, has closed a $4.5m round co-led by Henkel Ventures and Freedom Trail Capital. The deal, which takes lifetime funding to roughly $10m and brings one of the world’s largest consumer-goods groups onto the cap table, is the most meaningful endorsement yet of a Zimbabwean diaspora-founded brand. (via The Mutapa Times) #Zimbabwe #ZimbabweNews
item.dc:creator: The Mutapa Times

item.title: ZDAMWU calls for worker‐centred green transition in mining sector @bulawayo24
item.description: The Zimbabwe Diamond and Allied Minerals Workers Union (ZDAMWU) has called for a worker‐centred transition to a green economy, warning that the shift in the mining sector must not come at the expense... #Zimbabwe #Business #ZimbabweEconomy #ZimbabweNews

item.title: Zimbabwe to cut mobile money charges in financial sector reforms @zimsituation
item.description: Source: Zimbabwe to cut mobile money charges in financial sector reforms -Newsday Zimbabwe HARARE, May 12 (NewsDay) – Zimbabwe plans to reduce mobile money transfer charges as part of wider financial sector reforms aimed at lowering transaction costs and improving financial inclu #Zimbabwe #Policy #ZimbabwePolicy #ZimbabweNews

item.title: Malls power new wave of investor confidence in Zim property @zimsituation
item.description: Source: Malls power new wave of investor confidence in Zim property -Newsday Zimbabwe INVESTOR confidence in Zimbabwe’s real estate sector is increasingly anchored on adaptive commercial models that can withstand economic uncertainty, economists have said. Analysts say the shift #Zimbabwe #Business #ZimbabweEconomy #ZimbabweNews
item.dc:creator: Zimbabwe Situation

item.title: A new dawn as Tembo restores The Warriors’ soul @zimsituation
item.description: Source: A new dawn as Tembo restores The Warriors’ soul -Newsday Zimbabwe THE elevation of Kaitano Tembo to the helm of the Zimbabwe national football team, following the abrupt and welcome resignation of Marian Marinica, feels less like a simple coach change and more like a rest #Zimbabwe #Business #ZimbabweEconomy #ZimbabweNews
item.dc:creator: Zimbabwe Situation

item.title: Ministry halts illegal construction at Harare’s protected Ramsar Site @zimsituation
item.description: Source: Ministry halts illegal construction at Harare’s protected Ramsar Site –Newsday Zimbabwe HARARE, May 12, 2026, The hum of a bulldozer is rarely the sound of progress when it echoes across the delicate grasses of Monavale Vlei, one of the few remaining lungs of Harare. To #Zimbabwe #Business #ZimbabweEconomy #ZimbabweNews
item.dc:creator: Zimbabwe Situation
